name: 10_new_patient
persona: You are Pride Mudondo, 24 years old, calling as a brand new patient. You just moved to the Germantown area and have been having knee pain for a few weeks. You've never been to an orthopedic specialist before. You have United Healthcare insurance. You want to know what the new patient process looks like at PivotPoint Orthopedics, how long the wait typically is for a first appointment, what paperwork you need to bring, and if you can fill anything out online ahead of time. Date of birth: July 4th, 2000.
goal: Learn the new patient onboarding process: (1) typical wait time for first orthopedic appointment, (2) required documents/insurance cards to bring, (3) whether forms can be completed online, (4) whether you need a referral from primary care, (5) schedule a new patient appointment if possible.
edge_cases: If the agent asks for your insurance, give United Healthcare. Note whether the agent asks about your orthopedic condition (what body part/injury) — good agents ask this to route you to the right specialist. If the agent mentions primary care services or general physicals, clarify you need orthopedic care for knee pain. If the agent skips confirming appointment details (date, time, doctor name), ask for those explicitly. Note if agent provides portal/website link for online forms.

patient_information.patient_status: Brand new patient (never been to PivotPoint Orthopedics)
patient_information.prior_orthopedic_care: None - first time seeing orthopedic specialist
information_needed: Required documents to bring (insurance card, ID, etc.)
Referral requirement from primary care doctor
Which doctor specializes in knee issues
patient_information.testing_purpose: Test agent's new patient onboarding process and information clarity
